attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

file.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
    <parent>
        <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
        <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
        <version>4.0.6</version>
        <relativePath/> <!-- lookup parent from repository -->
    </parent>
    <groupId>com.smart</groupId>
    <artifactId>smartoa</artifactId>
    <version>0.0.1-SNAPSHOT</version>
    <name>smartoa</name>
    <description>smartoa</description>
    <url/>
    <licenses>
        <license/>
    </licenses>
    <developers>
        <developer/>
    </developers>
    <scm>
        <connection/>
        <developerConnection/>
        <tag/>
        <url/>
    </scm>
    <properties>
        <java.version>21</java.version>
    </properties>
    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-webmvc</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-thymeleaf</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.h2database</groupId>
            <artifactId>h2</artifactId>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.mysql</groupId>
            <artifactId>mysql-connector-j</artifactId>
            <scope>runtime</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
            <optional>true</optional>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-webmvc-test</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <plugins>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
                <artifactId>spring-boot-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <excludes>
                        <exclude>
                            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
                            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
                        </exclude>
                    </excludes>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>default-compile</id>
                        <phase>compile</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>compile</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <annotationProcessorPaths>
                                <path>
                                    <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
                                    <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
                                </path>
                            </annotationProcessorPaths>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                    <execution>
                        <id>default-testCompile</id>
                        <phase>test-compile</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>testCompile</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <annotationProcessorPaths>
                                <path>
                                    <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
                                    <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
                                </path>
                            </annotationProcessorPaths>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

</project>

file.content:
spring.application.name=smartoa

# H2
spring.datasource.url=jdbc:h2:mem:smartoa
spring.datasource.driver-class-name=org.h2.Driver
spring.datasource.username=sa
spring.datasource.password=
spring.h2.console.enabled=true

# JPA
spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update
spring.jpa.show-sql=true
spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.format_sql=true
spring.jpa.defer-datasource-initialization=true

# Thymeleaf
spring.thymeleaf.cache=false
spring.thymeleaf.prefix=classpath:/templates/
spring.thymeleaf.suffix=.html

# SQL init
spring.sql.init.mode=always

content.thinking: Let me read the main Java files to understand the project better.
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of this project. Let me also check the DTOs and repository files quickly.

content.text:
这是一个 **SmartOA** — 简易办公自动化（OA）请假管理系统，基于 **Spring Boot 4.0.6** + **Java 21** 构建。

---

## 技术栈

| 层面 | 技术 |
|------|------|
| 框架 | Spring Boot 4.0.6 (Spring MVC + Spring Data JPA) |
| 模板引擎 | Thymeleaf |
| 数据库 | H2 内存数据库（开发阶段），可选 MySQL |
| 工具 | Lombok、Bean Validation |

---

## 核心功能

这是一个 **请假申请与审批** 系统，包含两个角色：

### 1. 员工 (EMPLOYEE)
- 登录后进入员工面板，查看自己的请假记录
- 提交新的请假申请（选择请假类型、起止日期、填写原因）
- 查看某条请假单的审批详情

### 2. 经理 (MANAGER)
- 登录后进入经理面板，查看所有待审批的请假单
- 点击某条请假单进入审批页，选择 **批准 (APPROVE)** 或 **驳回 (REJECT)**，并可填写批注

---

## 项目结构

```
src/main/java/com/smartoa/
├── SmartoaApplication.java        # Spring Boot 启动类
├── controller/
│   ├── UserController.java        # /api/login、/api/logout（REST 接口）
│   ├── LeaveController.java       # /api/leave/submit、/api/leave/approve（REST 接口）
│   └── PageController.java        # 页面路由（/login、/employee/...、/manager/...）
├── service/
│   ├── UserService.java           # 登录校验、从 session 获取当前用户
│   └── LeaveService.java          # 提交请假、审批请假、查询请假单
├── entity/
│   ├── User.java                  # 用户实体（sys_user 表）
│   ├── LeaveRequest.java          # 请假单实体（leave_request 表）
│   └── ApprovalRecord.java       # 审批记录实体（approval_record 表）
├── dto/
│   ├── LoginDTO.java              # 登录请求 DTO
│   └── LeaveSubmitDTO.java        # 请假提交 DTO
└── repository/
    ├── UserRepository.java        # 按用户名查用户
    ├── LeaveRequestRepository.java # 按申请人/状态查请假单
    └── ApprovalRecordRepository.java # 按请假单查审批记录
```

---

## 数据库表关系

- **sys_user** — 用户表，有 `role` 字段区分 EMPLOYEE / MANAGER
- **leave_request** — 请假单表，关联申请人 (`applicant_id`)，状态流转：`PENDING` → `APPROVED` / `REJECTED`
- **approval_record** — 审批记录表，关联请假单和审批人

预设了 3 个测试用户（`data.sql`）：
- `admin` / `123456` — 经理（王经理）
- `zhangsan` / `123456` — 员工（张三）
- `lisi` / `123456` — 员工（李四）

---

## 业务流程

1. 用户访问 `/login` 页面，输入用户名密码登录
2. 登录信息存入 `HttpSession`，根据角色跳转到不同面板
3. 员工在面板提交请假申请 → 状态为 `PENDING`
4. 经理在面板看到待审批列表 → 点击进入审批页 → 批准或驳回
5. 员工可在详情页查看审批结果和审批记录

---

总结：这是一个典型的 Spring Boot 入门级学习项目，覆盖了 **MVC 三层架构、JPA 数据访问、Thymeleaf 模板渲染、Session 鉴权** 等基础知识点，适合用来学习 Java Web 开发。

content: 刚刚给你完整讲解了 SmartOA 项目——一个基于 Spring Boot 的请假审批系统，包含员工提交请假和经理审批两大功能。你可以直接问想了解的具体部分，或者告诉我接下来要做什么。 (disable recaps in /config)

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user asked "给我讲一下这个项目" (Explain this project to me). The intent was to get a comprehensive overview of the SmartOA project — its purpose, architecture, tech stack, and code structure. This was a purely informational request with no implementation or modification task.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Spring Boot 4.0.6 with Java 21
   - Spring MVC (REST controllers + page controllers with Thymeleaf)
   - Spring Data JPA with Hibernate for ORM
   - H2 in-memory database for development (with MySQL driver available for production)
   - Thymeleaf server-side template rendering
   - Lombok for boilerplate reduction
   - HttpSession-based authentication (not Spring Security)
   - Role-based access: EMPLOYEE (submit leave, view own records) and MANAGER (approve/reject leave requests)
   - Standard MVC three-layer architecture: Controller → Service → Repository
   - JPA entity lifecycle callbacks (@PrePersist, @PreUpdate) for timestamps
   - DTO pattern for request body mapping
   - SQL seed data initialization via data.sql

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `pom.xml`
     - Defines project dependencies and build config. Key: Spring Boot 4.0.6 parent, spring-boot-starter-webmvc, spring-boot-starter-data-jpa, spring-boot-starter-thymeleaf, spring-boot-starter-validation, H2, MySQL-connector-j, Lombok.
   - `src/main/resources/application.properties`
     - Configures H2 in-memory DB (jdbc:h2:mem:smartoa), JPA ddl-auto=update, Thymeleaf cache disabled, SQL init mode=always.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/SmartoaApplication.java`
     - Standard Spring Boot entry point with @SpringBootApplication.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/entity/User.java`
     - Entity mapped to `sys_user` table. Fields: id, username (unique), password, realName, role (EMPLOYEE/MANAGER), department. Uses Lombok @Data, @NoArgsConstructor, @AllArgsConstructor.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/entity/LeaveRequest.java`
     - Entity mapped to `leave_request`. Fields: id, applicant (@ManyToOne to User), leaveType, startDate, endDate, reason, status (PENDING/APPROVED/REJECTED), createTime, updateTime. Has @PrePersist and @PreUpdate hooks for timestamps.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/entity/ApprovalRecord.java`
     - Entity mapped to `approval_record`. Fields: id, leaveRequest (@ManyToOne), approver (@ManyToOne to User), action (APPROVE/REJECT), comment, createTime. @PrePersist for createTime.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/dto/LoginDTO.java`
     - Simple DTO with username and password fields.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/dto/LeaveSubmitDTO.java`
     - DTO with leaveType, startDate, endDate, reason fields.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/repository/UserRepository.java`
     - JPA repository with `Optional<User> findByUsername(String username)`.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/repository/LeaveRequestRepository.java`
     - JPA repository with `findByApplicantIdOrderByCreateTimeDesc`, `findByStatusOrderByCreateTimeDesc`, `findByStatusNotOrderByCreateTimeDesc`.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/repository/ApprovalRecordRepository.java`
     - JPA repository with `findByLeaveRequestIdOrderByCreateTimeAsc`.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/service/UserService.java`
     - `login(username, password)`: Finds user by username, compares password (plain text comparison — no encryption).
     - `getLoginUser(HttpSession session)`: Retrieves userId from session, finds User entity.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/service/LeaveService.java`
     - `submitLeave(applicantId, LeaveSubmitDTO)`: Creates LeaveRequest with status "PENDING".
     - `approveLeave(requestId, approverId, action, comment)`: Checks status is PENDING, updates to APPROVED/REJECTED, creates ApprovalRecord.
     - `getMyRequests`, `getPendingRequests`, `getRequestDetail`, `getApprovalRecords`: Query methods.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/controller/UserController.java` (@RestController)
     - `POST /api/login`: Authenticates user, stores userId in session, returns redirect URL based on role.
     - `GET /api/logout`: Invalidates session.
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/controller/LeaveController.java` (@RestController)
     - `POST /api/leave/submit`: Submits leave request (requires login).
     - `POST /api/leave/approve`: Approves/rejects leave (requires login + MANAGER role).
   - `src/main/java/com/smartoa/controller/PageController.java` (@Controller)
     - Routes for `/`, `/login`, `/employee/dashboard`, `/employee/detail/{id}`, `/manager/dashboard`, `/manager/approve/{id}`. Each checks login via session and role authorization.
   - `src/main/resources/data.sql`
     - Inserts 3 test users: admin (MANAGER, 技术部), zhangsan (EMPLOYEE, 技术部), lisi (EMPLOYEE, 产品部), all with password "123456".
   - `src/main/resources/templates/` — 5 Thymeleaf HTML templates (login, employee/dashboard, employee/detail, manager/dashboard, manager/approve). Not read but listed.

4. Errors and fixes:
   No errors were encountered during this conversation. The task was purely exploratory and all file reads succeeded.

5. Problem Solving:
   The user asked for a project overview. I solved this by systematically reading the pom.xml, application configuration, all entity classes, all service classes, all controller classes, all DTOs, all repositories, and the seed data SQL. I then synthesized this into a structured explanation covering tech stack, core features (employee leave submission + manager approval), project architecture, database schema/relationships, and business flow.

6. All user messages:
   - "给我讲一下这个项目" (Explain this project to me) — the only user message in this conversation.

7. Pending Tasks:
   None. The user's request was fully addressed.

8. Current Work:
   The conversation was a single-turn Q&A. The user asked me to explain the project, and I responded with a comprehensive analysis after reading all 21 source files. The explanation covered: the project is SmartOA, a Spring Boot 4.0.6 leave-request management system with employee (submit leave) and manager (approve/reject) roles, using H2 in-memory DB, JPA, Thymeleaf, and HttpSession-based auth. No code was modified; no ongoing work remains.

9. Optional Next Step:
   None. The user's request was purely informational and was fully completed. There are no pending tasks or next steps to take.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\28421\.claude\projects\F--IDEA-daima-xuexijava-smartoa\d8bcda30-71c5-4337-a249-9f8478526713.jsonl

<local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
